title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which one of these am I actually meant to be saving?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Hmmm... neither of them were at the previous council meeting.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: The goblins get through leaders so fast I find it hard to keep track. I heard the last leader died during a game of pin the tail on the basilisk.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Well, no worries. I'm sure if you just find something that they'll both eat then that should work. I hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remembering the last time I dealt with these two, that may be easier said than done.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Well you could try asking some of the other goblins in their village for ideas. You should be able to find it just to the North of Falador, unless they've decided the sky is the wrong colour there or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we talk about what happened in the dining room?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Wow! You were incredible! The way you defeated the Culinaromancer, you made it seem so easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I don't suppose you can remember how I defeated him can you?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Of course! Which bit can't you remember doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those bickering goblins... How did I protect them again?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, that was a weird one alright, you told me that they couldn't agree on what food they liked, so you ended up just mushing a bunch of stuff together somehow which seemed to do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?}}

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask about what food you like?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to save you from the Culinaromancer's spell.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: From who's what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At the council meeting in Lumbridge, there was a Culinaromancer who tried to cast an evil spell on you!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Lumbridge...?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Oh yeah. Me remember. Me was goblin representy thingy.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: No you weren't! That was me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind that, don't you remember the spell?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: All me remember is Wartface stealin' chair!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Weren't your chair. You shouldn' even've been there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: I there before you were. So I get chair...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Market research.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: But us never use your markets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly. That's why we need to find out what goblins eat, so we can better cater to your needs.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Go away human, we busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it'll only take a minute of your time.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: I said we busy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... no particular reason.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Me see no particular reason to answer you then.

sections.title: Talking to a Goblin Village goblin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I need your help. Wartface and Bentnoze have been cursed!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: What that got to do with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find a meal they'd both eat.

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me no chef, ask village cook instead.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Go away human, me's busy here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Busy doing what?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Me's working on new way of cooking. Will be most famous-est goblin cook ever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this new way of cooking?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Got the idea from the dwarves. Saw them playing with something. They called it a malty canned nun or summat. Used lotsa heat and bright light.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Wouldn't tell Mudknuckles how it worked of course. But I figured it out. Mudknuckles is smarter than them. Now I'm gonna be able to cook better'n anyone. And faster too, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: No luck. Will work cos Mudknuckles destined for great things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help. Wartface and Bentnoze have been trapped by a terrible curse!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Oh, maybe that why Mudknuckles not been shouted at recently.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Hmmm, s'pose me should help. But first Mudknuckles must finish experiment. Unfortunately is missing one vital ingredient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need? Maybe I can get it for you.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Is called charcoal. Mudknuckles hear you can get it from shops on Karamja, but the customs officers, they no like goblins any more.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Burnt trees, they good too. Smash 'em up with axes. But Mudknuckles only knows of burnt trees in the wilderness, and Mudknuckles too afraid to go there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'll come back later then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess I'll leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Yes, Mudknuckles too busy to talk.

sections.title: Talking to the goblin cook again with charcoal in inventory

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Go away human, me's busy here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Busy doing what?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Me's working on new way of cooking. Will be most famous-est goblin cook ever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the charcoal you were after.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Really? Then no time to lose. Mudknuckles already have everything else. Sulphur 'n stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sulphur? Er...

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Must be moving. Follow me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure this is a good idea?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Absolutely, best idea Mudknuckles ever had.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm really not convinced that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should it shake like that?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: It fine, it...

lines.speaker: Goblin 1
lines.text: What that noise?

lines.speaker: Goblin 1
lines.text: You just hear that?

lines.speaker: Goblin 2
lines.text: Hear what?

lines.speaker: Goblin 1
lines.text: Now there shaking too!

lines.speaker: Goblin 1
lines.text: Ah. That'd be Mudknuckles again then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mudknuckles?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: It...

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: It worked!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It did... ?!

sections.title: Talking to the burnt goblin cook

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: You the human who helped me. Mudknuckles is very grateful, will be famous real soon now. Is just collecting results. And so many results! On walls, on ceiling, everywhere!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: I thinking of calling it fast food, 'cos of how fast it goes from raw to burnt, see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds like a great idea.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: You think so? Everyone else think Mudknuckles crazy. Seem think explosive cooking might be dangerous or summat.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Can't see why. Not like you gonna get food poisoning. Food is very well cooked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes... I'm sure food poisoning was what they were worried about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think that will sell? Like you said, it's burnt.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: So maybe some imperfections, but fast food will sell. People buy anything when in hurry, even if does taste like sawdust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help...

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Yes, yes, Mudknuckles very grateful, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your leaders, Wartface and Bentnoze, have been trapped by a terrible curse!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: So that's why Mudknuckles not been shouted at recently. But how can I be helping? Cooking and magic no mix.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Tried that before, Mudknuckles had some very angry wizards at door after trying to burn staffs to cook on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to create a dish that both of the Generals would eat to save them from the curse.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Is impossible. Mudknuckles try it before, but their requests, they make no sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what did they want?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Well Bentnoze wanted fruit in the dish. But Wartface, only fruit he eat is sliced oranges.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound too bad.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Maybe, but Bentnoze objects to the colour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Wartface, he want some nice juicy maggots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get maggots?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Humans use them all the time for fishing. Mudknuckles think this a crazy waste of maggots mind, but each to their own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Each to their own indeed, but what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Bentnoze not like maggots, say they're too bland. Would only eat them if they spicier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, is there anything other than orange slices and maggots?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Bentnoze asked for some bread in the mix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess, Wartface complained right?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Yep, Wartface think bread too crunchy. He prefer slimier soggier food, like the maggots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let me get this straight. I need orange slices which aren't orange, maggots that aren't bland, and bread which isn't crunchy?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Yep, 'bout sums it up. Bring 'em all to me and Mudknuckles will make suitable dish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wonderful. Well, I guess I'll have to see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to your experimentation then.

sections.title: Talking to the burnt goblin cook again

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: You the human who helped me. Mudknuckles is very grateful, will be famous real soon now. Is just collecting results. And so many results! On walls, on ceiling, everywhere!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: I thinking of calling it fast food, 'cos of how fast it goes from raw to burnt, see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help. About that dish that both Wartface and Bentnoze would eat...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was the problem with the orange slices?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Well Bentnoze wanted fruit in the dish. But Wartface, only fruit he eat is sliced oranges, and Bentnoze object to the colour of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did Bentnoze object to the maggots?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Bentnoze not like maggots, say they're too bland. Would only eat them if they spicier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did Wartface have against bread?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Wartface think bread too crunchy. He prefer his food to be soggier.

sections.title: Talking to the burnt goblin cook with all three ingredients in inventory

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: You the human who helped me. Mudknuckles is very grateful, will be famous real soon now. Is just collecting results. And so many results! On walls, on ceiling, everywhere!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: I thinking of calling it fast food, 'cos of how fast it goes from raw to burnt, see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the ingredients we need to make a meal for Wartface and Bentnoze.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Mudknuckles is confused. Ingredients make no sense. But give 'em here and Mudknuckles make what you need.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: There you go, but don't go blaming Mudknuckles if doesn't work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks!

sections.title: Talking to the burnt goblin cook after receiving the slop of compromise

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: You the human who helped me. Mudknuckles is very grateful, will be famous real soon now. Is just collecting results. And so many results! On walls, on ceiling, everywhere!

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: I thinking of calling it fast food, 'cos of how fast it goes from raw to burnt, see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help. Are you sure Wartface and Bentnoze will both eat this dish we've made?

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Highly unlikely, but is best Mudknuckles can come up with am afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help. I lost the dish you gave to me to save Wartface and Bentnoze.

lines.speaker: Goblin Cook
lines.text: Is ok, Mudknuckles make plenty. Take some more.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Good work adventurer, I've teleported them away to safety! You only have [number] council members left to protect!
